sub_pixel_system.notes: Puzzle game; sub-pixel precision not documented

special_mechanics.name: block_gravity
special_mechanics.description: In Tetris: falling tetrominoes descend; players can hold down to make them fall faster

special_mechanics.name: pill_gravity
special_mechanics.description: In Dr. Mario: colored pills descend; players can press down to drop faster

special_mechanics.name: block_rotation
special_mechanics.description: Players can rotate tetrominoes to place them in specific spots

special_mechanics.name: pill_rotation
special_mechanics.description: Colored pills can be rotated around their center axis

tier_notes: Tier 2 (T2) - empirical/qualitative data. This is a puzzle game compilation; movement physics are not applicable. Game mechanics are tile/grid-based with falling piece gravity and player-controlled acceleration of fall speed. No platformer movement parameters documented.
